prompt: Create a horizontal framed paper-cut shadow box artwork mounted on a pale gray wall, with a light natural wood frame and a deep black inner recess casting realistic layered shadows. Inside the box, depict a moonlit desert dance scene made from stacked cut paper and foam layers: two anime-inspired belly dancers in flowing blue and gold costumes, one female dancer on the left facing inward with long dark brown ponytail, glasses, bare midriff, raised left arm and extended right arm, and one male dancer on the right turned partly away with short brown hair, bare back, raised right arm and extended left arm. Both dancers wear layered sapphire, navy, and pale blue fabric panels, translucent gauzy sleeve ribbons, gold trim, jewelry, anklets, and bare feet, with the ribbons curling outward as separate cut-paper layers. The background is a deep navy night sky with exactly one large pale full moon near the upper center and 16 small white stars scattered around it, above layered beige and gray desert dunes. Emphasize a handcrafted 3D paper diorama look, clean anime silhouettes, crisp cut edges, soft drop shadows between layers, elegant motion, balanced composition, museum-like craft photography, realistic wall lighting, high detail, no text. Use light natural wood, sapphire blue, gold, moonlit desert, and anime paper-cut shadow box.
